title: Nephrodium securidiforme
taxonomy: Nephrodium securidiforme is classified as a species of fern, belonging to the family Matoniaceae. This plant is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The genus Nephrodium was previously recognized but is now considered a synonym of the genus Phanerosorus. Phanerosorus securidiforme is the current accepted name for this plant. The classification reflects the ongoing efforts to reorganize and refine the taxonomy of ferns.
uses: Nephrodium securidiforme, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Nephrodium securidiforme, also known as the Shield Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
